summary: Tested Price Manager feature (iteration 167). All 26 backend tests pass (100%). Frontend: all key UI elements verified. Fixed a critical product search bug in PriceManagerPage.js where (a) the wrong query param 'q' was used instead of 'search', and (b) res.data was treated as an array instead of {products: [...]} dict, causing TypeError and 'Search failed' toast. After fix, product search, product cards, audit prompts, tabs, scheme checkboxes, smart fill, and save button all working correctly.

integration_issues.issue: FIXED: Frontend used wrong param 'q' (API expects 'search') AND called .filter() on res.data dict instead of res.data.products array → TypeError → 'Search failed' toast. Fixed in PriceManagerPage.js doSearch function.

critical_code_review_comments: Bug fixed: PriceManagerPage.js doSearch used params: { q: searchQ } (backend expects 'search') and (res.data || []).filter() (res.data is an object {products:[...]}, not an array). Fixed to: params: { search: searchQ } and res.data?.products || []

Capital & Price Setup audit shows 'All products have capital set' and 'No low-margin issues' - expected behavior per context note since all products have capital in this test env. No 'Load first 30' buttons shown (correct when count=0).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Price Manager feature fully tested and working. Product search bug was fixed (wrong param name + wrong response field access). All backend endpoints: price-audit-summary, bulk-price-update, branch-prices/bulk-update, permissions/modules, permissions/presets/manager|cashier all pass. Frontend: nav-price-manager, pm-tab-prices, pm-tab-capital, pm-product-search, scheme-check-{key} checkboxes, smart-fill-apply-btn, pm-save-btn all present with correct data-testid. Capital & Price Setup audit prompts load correctly (both show 0 count which is correct for this env).

rca_of_issue.root_cause: Two bugs in PriceManagerPage.js doSearch callback: (1) API param 'q' instead of 'search' - backend ignores 'q' so no filtering but returns all products. (2) `(res.data || []).filter(...)` fails because res.data is `{products: [...], total: N, skip: N, limit: N}` (object, not array) - calling .filter() on an object throws TypeError caught by the catch block showing 'Search failed' toast.
rca_of_issue.fix_applied: Changed params to use 'search' key and changed res.data to Array.isArray(res.data) ? res.data : (res.data?.products || [])
